title: Price Elasticity Simulator
description: Industrial-grade tool to simulate price elasticity of demand, compute revenue impact, and identify hidden loss drivers. Aligned with ISO 9001, Lean Six Sigma, and WERC DC metrics.

inputs.label: Current Price per Unit
inputs.businessContext: The current selling price of the product in USD.

businessContext_i18n.en: The current selling price of the product in USD.

inputs.label: Current Quantity Sold (units/month)
inputs.businessContext: Monthly sales volume at current price.

businessContext_i18n.en: Monthly sales volume at current price.

inputs.label: Proposed Price Change (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage change in price (negative for decrease, positive for increase).

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage change in price (negative for decrease, positive for increase).

inputs.label: Price Elasticity of Demand (PED)
inputs.businessContext: Estimated elasticity coefficient (typically negative). Absolute value >1 indicates elastic demand.

businessContext_i18n.en: Estimated elasticity coefficient (typically negative). Absolute value >1 indicates elastic demand.

inputs.label: Variable Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Direct variable cost per unit (materials, labor, etc.).

businessContext_i18n.en: Direct variable cost per unit (materials, labor, etc.).

inputs.label: Fixed Cost per Month
inputs.businessContext: Total fixed costs allocated per month.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total fixed costs allocated per month.

inputs.label: Demand Shift Factor (seasonality/market)
inputs.businessContext: Multiplier to adjust baseline demand (e.g., 1.2 for 20% market growth).

businessContext_i18n.en: Multiplier to adjust baseline demand (e.g., 1.2 for 20% market growth).

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Quality of input data: low = ±20% error, medium = ±10%, high = ±5%.

businessContext_i18n.en: Quality of input data: low = ±20% error, medium = ±10%, high = ±5%.

inputs.label: Apply Six Sigma Process Adjustment
inputs.businessContext: If true, applies a 1.5 sigma shift to account for process variation (DMAIC).

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, applies a 1.5 sigma shift to account for process variation (DMAIC).

rules.message: Price elasticity of demand must be negative (law of demand).
Price change percentage out of realistic bounds.
Variable cost must be less than current price to maintain positive contribution margin.
Current quantity must be greater than zero.

thresholds.message: Revenue decline exceeds 5% — consider alternative pricing strategy.
Profit margin below 10% — risk of unprofitability.
Extremely elastic demand — small price changes cause large volume swings.

formulas.name: New Price
formulas.description: Calculated new price after applying percentage change.

formulas.name: Quantity Change Percent
formulas.description: Percentage change in quantity demanded using elasticity.

formulas.name: New Quantity
formulas.description: Forecasted quantity after price change, adjusted for demand shift and optional Six Sigma adjustment.

formulas.name: Current Revenue
formulas.description: Revenue at current price and quantity.

formulas.name: New Revenue
formulas.description: Revenue at new price and forecasted quantity.

formulas.name: Revenue Change Percent
formulas.description: Primary result: percentage change in revenue.

primary.label: Revenue Change (%)
primary.description: Percentage change in total revenue after price adjustment.

profit_margin_percent.label: Profit Margin (%)
profit_margin_percent.description: New profit as percentage of new revenue.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Elasticity Misestimation
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If actual elasticity differs from input, revenue impact may be significantly worse.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Fixed Cost Leverage
hiddenLossDrivers.description: High fixed costs amplify profit swings from volume changes.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Competitive Response
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Price change may trigger competitor reactions, altering demand beyond model.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Inventory Holding Cost
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Unexpected demand shifts may increase inventory carrying costs (WERC DC metrics).

suggestedActions.label: Validate Elasticity with A/B Test
suggestedActions.description: Run a controlled price experiment to confirm elasticity coefficient before full rollout.

suggestedActions.label: Segment Customers by Elasticity
suggestedActions.description: Apply differential pricing to low-elasticity segments to maximize revenue.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Variable Cost via Lean
suggestedActions.description: Use Lean Six Sigma tools (VSM, Kaizen) to lower variable cost and protect margin.

suggestedActions.label: Monitor Competitive Pricing
suggestedActions.description: Set up alerts for competitor price changes to adjust strategy dynamically.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Confidence-Adjusted Revenue Change (%)
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Revenue change adjusted for data confidence level (low: ±20%, medium: ±10%, high: ±5%).
